medical_history.history_of_present_illness: The patient presents with new elevations in ALT and AST. He had a transplant in ___. His course has been complicated by a transient rise in transaminases in ___ with ALT of 171 and AST of 74. He has had one week of fatigue, but no other symptoms consistent with infection. New labs yesterday showed an elevation in his AST and ALT to 233 and 394 from 79 and 61 respectively.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Elevated PSA being followed by Dr. ___; HBV cirrhosis s/p liver transplant in ___.
medical_history.family_history: Parents have a history of liver cancer. He has 5 siblings: 3 brothers died from liver cancer; 2 sisters alive, not screened for CAHBV or liver cancer.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vitals: Temp 97.3F, BP 111/71, HR 69, RR 18, O2-sat 100% RA. Well-appearing man in NAD, comfortable, lying in bed.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Awake, A&Ox3, CNs II-XII grossly intact. Lungs: CTA bilat. Heart: Normal Rate Regular Rhythm, no MRG, nl S1-S2. Abdomen: NABS, soft/NT/ND, no masses or HSM, no rebound/guarding, large, transabdominal and longitudinal mid abdominal scar, well healed. Skin: Few petechiae on his chest.
